title: Multimedia Journalism
catalogDescription: Teaches students how to write for the media, including print, online and broadcast media. Includes writing for public relations. Stresses newswriting mechanics, learning news style and newsroom terminology. Instructor encourages students to enroll in JOURN 104 and join the staff of The Current, Green River's print and online publication.

title: Introduction to Public Relations
catalogDescription: Provides an overview of the field of public relations from history to practice. Introduces writing, interviewing and publicity techniques and creating press kits and news releases for a variety of audiences.

title: Newswriting
catalogDescription: Writing for news media; clarity in use of language, style and punctuation; sentence and paragraph structure and organization of stories; credibility of information and news judgment; basic methods of researching and writing. Weekly news discussions based on current events.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101  or instructor consent.
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Advanced Newswriting
catalogDescription: Advanced writing for news media; clarity in use of language, style and punctuation; sentence and paragraph structure and organization of stories; credibility of information and news judgment; basic methods of researching and writing. Weekly news discussions based on current events.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: JOURN 104 with a grade of 1.0 or higher; or instructor consent.

catalogDescription: Introduces students to the art of storytelling, stressing techniques such as narrative journalism, conversational interviewing, and use of natural sound and video sequence construction.

title: Mobile and Social Media Journalism
catalogDescription: Critically examines how journalists and news organizations are using emerging forms of social media and mobile platforms. Students gain hands-on experience by experimenting with social media and mobile devices for news gathering, distribution, and audience engagement. Students will also learn how to use analytic tools to monitor and analyze the effectiveness of their mobile and social media activity.

title: Multimedia Writing and Creation
catalogDescription: In this hands-on course, students explore the basics of multimedia creation through projects and workshops. They learn an introduction to the theory and practice of podcasting, streaming, radio and TV broadcasting, social media content creation, script writing, and news reporting.

title: Advanced Multimedia Writing and Creation
catalogDescription: In this hands-on course, students delve into the theory and practice of multimedia creation through projects and workshops. Building on foundational knowledge, they explore intermediate concepts in podcasting, streaming, radio and TV broadcasting, social media content creation, script writing, and news reporting.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: JOURN 115 or instructor consent.

title: Podcasting Laboratory
catalogDescription: This hands-on course instructs students on how to take an idea for a podcast and make it a reality. Interviewing techniques and strategies for gaining an audience are emphasized. Students learn about developing narratives and how different styles of podcasts are produced while working hands-on with equipment and software. Instruction in podcast promotion, distribution and monetization is also featured.

title: Audio Editing for Digital Media
catalogDescription: Students are introduced to the fundamentals, technology and methods used in the audio production and broadcast industry. Students use Adobe Audition to learn audio recording and editing techniques for creating professional multitrack media products such as podcasts, interviews, radio promotions and music.

title: Introduction to Broadcasting & Digital Media
catalogDescription: Offers the student an overview of the broadcast and digital media industry from the origin of radio to the domination of television. Continues with the growth of cable TV, satellite radio & TV, as well as digital media and social platforms.

catalogDescription: Covers radio management practices emphasizing the business of broadcasting. Includes sales, promotion, community relations, government regulations, demographics, programming, and modern applications such as digital delivery and social media usage.

title: Sports Broadcasting and Play-by-Play Announcing
catalogDescription: This course immerses students in the dynamic world of sports broadcasting and play-by-play announcing, cultivating a deep appreciation for the art and science of broadcast and digital media. Emphasizing voice quality, articulation, enunciation, and pronunciation, students receive hands-on guidance to refine their skills. The curriculum covers the nuances of sports announcing for television, radio, and digital platforms.

title: Careers in Digital Media
catalogDescription: Examines careers in digital media such as social media manager, video journalist, content editor, multimedia producer, on-air personality and more. Students will learn the expectations and real-world demands to successfully perform and interact in these fast-paced, ever-changing fields. Students will learn about various job pathways and how to showcase their skills and knowledge to future employers.

title: Radio Management
catalogDescription: Students will learn additional skills as part of the management team at our broadcast radio stations (KGRG and KGRG-FM). Planning, organizing, managing events and staff are practiced as preparation for advanced careers in broadcasting.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Instructor consent.
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: JOURN 115 or instructor consent.
